summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a smokin' March 20 1991 show, highlighted by an explosive Eyes‑of‑the‑World > Foolish Heart transition and a second set that never wanted to end.
review: The recording is praised for its crystal‑clear sound – thanks to Charlie Miller’s excellent taping – making the performance easy to enjoy. Fans rave about a powerful first set and an even more incendiary second set, especially the Eyes of the World into Foolish Heart segue that “brings the house down.” Overall the show is regarded as one of the band’s post‑Brent highlights.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (4–5) and uniformly enthusiastic comments about both the music and the sound quality indicate a strong show with an excellent recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners describe the audio as “great” and “crisp”; no issues or dropouts are mentioned, suggesting a clean soundboard capture.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is called wonderful; second set is described as “off the hook,” with fans wishing it never ended, indicating a high‑energy, well‑paced performance.
